alerts.title: Iran's uranium enrichment accelerates; breakout window narrows to 1-2 weeks
alerts.summary: IAEA reports Iran maintains 60% U-235 enrichment with estimated breakout time of 1-2 weeks per global_metrics nuclear headlines. Multiple sources (Reuters, The Hill, JNS, Ynetnews) confirm inspectors unable to verify stockpile since conflict began, creating verification gap in nuclear oversight. Represents material acceleration of nuclear weapons capability during active hostilities.

alerts.title: Iranian Navy blocks Persian Gulf; third US destroyer repulsion in sustained blockade
alerts.summary: Iranian Navy reportedly targeted two Arleigh Burke-class destroyers (USS Truxton, USS Mason) with suicide drones and anti-ship missiles, forcing retreat to Sea of Oman per Telegram (ME Spectator, ~35K views). Third such blockade attempt indicates systematic Iranian strategy to deny US Gulf access and represent escalation of anti-access operations in strategic waterway.

alerts.title: US deploys third carrier strike group; USS Gerald R. Ford repositioned to Mediterranean
alerts.summary: Assets feed confirms USS Gerald R. Ford carrier positioned in eastern Mediterranean (34.24°N, 30.26°E) alongside USS Dwight D. Eisenhower in Red Sea and USS Abraham Lincoln in Arabian Sea. Three-carrier deployment represents largest US naval concentration in theater since conflict onset and signals sustained operational readiness for regional escalation.

alerts.title: Hezbollah targets IDF Northern Command; drone strike narrowly misses senior general
alerts.summary: Telegram (ME Spectator, ~47K views) reports Hezbollah FPV drone directly struck vehicle carrying Maj. Gen. Rafi Milo (IDF Northern Command) during southern Lebanon visit two weeks prior. General exited vehicle minutes before impact; incident demonstrates precision targeting capability against high-value military leadership and elevated threat to IDF command structure.

alerts.title: US media sentiment plummets to 57% negative; war-related keywords surge in coverage
alerts.summary: Sentiment analysis shows US news environment at 57% negative (36% positive) with 'trump' (81 mentions), 'putin' (13), 'ukraine' (10), 'war' (8), and 'strikes' (7) dominating discourse per sentiment feed. Sustained negative polarity reflects domestic political polarization over Iran-proxy conflict management and Ukraine support amid active Middle East escalation.

analysis_note: The conflict remains at critical threat level (9/10) with Iran pursuing nuclear acceleration, naval blockade of the Gulf, and proxy network activation while US sustains three-carrier presence; ceasefires are collapsing (Lebanon-Israel) and political pressure is mounting domestically.
